page: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-32 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the n1 family. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical Intel Xeon cores running at 2.3 GHz, with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 29.0 GB of RAM, resulting in a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of approximately 0.9 GB per core. It does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data reveals a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively weak, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 25%. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression register in the bottom 10%. Conversely, multi-core benchmarks, including stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU tests, consistently achieve average tier performance. Memory bandwidth and database operations also perform in the middle 50% range.

The primary tradeoff of this instance is its low memory density and weak single-threaded execution speed, making it inefficient for memory-heavy or single-core bound applications. However, the high vCPU count provides cost-efficient parallel processing capacity for multi-threaded workloads that do not require large memory footprints. This configuration is suitable for parallel software compilation, multi-threaded decompression, static web serving, and basic database operations.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-32 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 29.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.3 GHz across 16 physical cores, this instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark results indicate a significant performance divergence: single-core metrics, such as Geekbench single-core and single-threaded compression, rank in the bottom 25% and 10% respectively, while multi-core operations, including software compilation, ray tracing, and PassMark CPU tests, consistently perform in the average tier. With a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 0.9 GB per core, this instance offers a cost-efficient profile for parallelized workloads that are not memory-intensive. It is suited for multi-threaded tasks such as software compilation, static web serving, and parallel data processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-32 is an x86_64 server with 32 vCPUs and 29.0 GB RAM. It offers average multi-core performance but exhibits weak single-core speeds and low memory density.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highcpu-32 features 32 vCPUs, 29.0 GB RAM, and average multi-core performance, suitable for compute-heavy parallel tasks.
tagline: A high-vCPU, low-memory compute instance designed for parallelized, multi-threaded workloads that do not require extensive memory or local storage.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical Intel Xeon cores at 2.3 GHz
29.0 GB of system memory with no bundled local storage or GPUs
Average multi-core performance in software compilation and ray tracing
Weak single-core execution and low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 0.9 GB
Suited for multi-threaded workloads, static web serving, and parallel processing
